title: isss608-group10-project
text:
This project examines customer transaction behaviour within the Colombian fintech ecosystem using the COFINFAD dataset. The overall objective is to understand how socio-demographic and behavioural factors shape customer financial activity, digital engagement, and satisfaction outcomes.
The project combines multiple visual analytics approaches to study customer behaviour from different perspectives, including exploratory analysis, customer segmentation, and predictive modelling.



The objective of this project is to understand the behaviours behind customer transactions and how socio-demographic factors, such as age, income, education, and household characteristics, affect financial activity within the Colombian fintech ecosystem.
By applying visual analytics and statistical techniques, the project aims to identify meaningful customer patterns, detect behavioural differences across demographic groups, and generate insights that support better interpretation of fintech user behaviour.



This group project consists of three main analytical components:


This component investigates the relationship between socio-demographic characteristics and transaction behaviour. It focuses on descriptive statistics, exploratory visualizations, and confirmatory analysis to identify patterns in transaction count, average transaction value, and total transaction volume.



This component applies customer segmentation techniques to identify groups of users with similar behavioural, financial, and engagement profiles. Clustering helps reveal distinct customer segments such as mainstream users, high-value users, and digitally engaged users.



This component develops predictive models to examine two important outcomes within the Colombian fintech ecosystem: **customer satisfaction** and **customer transaction behaviour**. Using demographic, behavioural, and digital engagement variables, the analysis evaluates how well customer attributes explain both customer experience and financial activity on the platform.




The analysis uses the COFINFAD dataset, which contains customer-level and transaction-level data from a Colombian fintech environment.
The dataset includes: - socio-demographic attributes - behavioural and engagement variables - transaction frequency and value measures - customer satisfaction indicators - customer segmentation and financial activity features



This website documents the development of the project across multiple stages, including:

project proposal
methodology
findings
poster
meeting minutes
team contributions
Shiny application




This website is currently being developed as part of the ISSS608 Visual Analytics group project. Additional content, analysis outputs, and application components will be added progressively.



Each group member contributes an individual analytical component that supports the overall project objective:

Exploratory Analysis — socio-demographic correlation and transaction behaviour
Clustering Analysis — customer segmentation using behavioural and engagement variables
Predictive Modelling — modelling Customer Satisfaction and Transaction Behaviour


Developed for ISSS608 Visual Analytics.
